channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Copper price hits highest in over a year on Grasberg disruption, Fed outlook
item.description: Copper prices rose to their highest level in more than a year, driven by mounting global supply disruptions and expectations that US rate cuts could support industrial demand. The article says the rally was strengthened by Freeport-McMoRan declaring force majeure at its Grasberg mine in Indonesia, a major copper-producing asset.

item.title: Copper & nickel see record high this week
item.description: A commodity strategist discusses nickel and copper hitting record highs this week before dipping later in the session. The discussion attributes the rally to strong demand and elevated volatility in 2026.

item.title: Nickel Prices Off The Bottom
item.description: S&P Global reports that nickel prices have moved above US$10,000 per tonne and describes the outlook as positive. The piece indicates a recovery phase in nickel prices after earlier weakness.

item.title: Nickel prices, news and market analysis
item.description: Fastmarkets provides ongoing coverage of nickel prices, market trends, and volatility in the nickel market. The page is positioned as a hub for current pricing and market analysis rather than a single news report.

item.title: Copper Prices Pull Back After Strong Rally
item.description: Copper prices have seen a significant correction after reaching multi-year highs, pulling back amid shifting market dynamics. The pullback follows a strong rally driven by optimism over potential US-Iran peace agreements and rising industrial demand.

item.title: Copper Prices Hit a New Record High, but Is the Metals Trade 'Stretched'?
item.description: Copper prices climbed to a new record high, surpassing $13,000 per ton for the first time, raising concerns about whether the metals trade is becoming overextended. Analysts note that continued climbing could signal unsustainable market pressure.

item.title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
item.description: Argus reports that steel market participants expect a slower, lower-margin second half of 2026 after a supply-led rally in prices during the first half of the year. The page also provides steel commodity price coverage and market forecasts across related ferrous inputs and products.

item.title: Aluminium Price Today - Investing.com
item.description: The page shows the current aluminium futures price and points to recent market movements. It also references Reuters coverage about Comex aluminium premiums easing on bets that U.S. tariff policy may be rolled back, suggesting market sensitivity to trade-policy changes.

item.title: Copper Price Today | Live Price of Copper per Ounce
item.description: Live copper charts show the current price of copper at 13,603.00 USD per ton, with steady trading across major markets like New York, London, and Hong Kong. The price reflects ongoing volatility amid global economic uncertainties.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
item.description: The United States announced sanctions targeting an armed group aligned with illicit mineral activity in the Democratic Republic of Congo, along with related mining firms. The report is relevant to copper supply chains and production conditions in a major mining region, but it is primarily about sanctions rather than direct capacity changes.

item.title: Italian steelmaker Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali said it is now sourcing all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy. The report also says the company plans to build a hydroelectric plant and an electrolyzer at its main site in Aosta.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires 65 percent of Inox
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali completed the acquisition of a 65% stake in Com.steel Inox SpA for €24 million. The company said the purchase supports its upstream integration and circularity commitments.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Drop as Trump Plans to Roll Back Levies on Metal Products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell by up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange following a Financial Times report that President Donald Trump intends to reduce levies on metal products, citing consumer harm. The price drop reflects trader reactions to potential tariff reductions on aluminum and steel, with aluminum trading at $3,030 per ton as of 1:03 p.m. local time.

item.title: J.P. Morgan Forecasts Aluminum Price at $2,200/mt for Q2 2025 Amid Reduced Demand Growth
item.description: J.P. Morgan Research predicts an average aluminum price of $2,200 per metric ton in the second quarter of 2025, with global demand growth reduced to 1% year-over-year. This shift is expected to create a surplus of approximately 200,000 metric tons in 2025, while copper prices are forecast to average $8,300/mt in the same period.

item.title: Aluminum Futures Trade at 3,543.00 with Daily Range Analysis Amid Tariff Rollback Bets
item.description: Aluminum futures closed at 3,543.00, up from a previous close of 3,518.48, as traders react to U.S. tariff rollback expectations. Comex aluminum premiums beyond August have declined due to bets on reduced levies, with Reuters reporting price movements in premium contracts on the Comex exchange.

item.title: Aluminum Price Hits Monthly Level of 3,666.10, Up from Previous Month and Year
item.description: The monthly aluminum price reached 3,666.10, increasing from 3,599.85 last month and 2,448.79 one year ago. This represents a 1.84% rise from the previous month, highlighting a steady upward trend in aluminum pricing amid global market dynamics.

item.title: In the week ending on June 13, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,854,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.3 percent
item.description: AISI’s latest weekly industry data reports U.S. domestic raw steel production at 1,854,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 80.3 percent. This provides the most direct recent snapshot of U.S. steel production and capacity usage.

item.title: In the week ending on June 13, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,854,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.3 percent.
item.description: AISI's weekly industry data shows U.S. domestic raw steel production at 1,854,000 net tons for the week ending June 13, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.3%. This provides the most recent production-and-capacity snapshot in the supplied results.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali finalizza l'acquisizione di Mannesmann Stainless Tubes
item.description: Cogne says it has completed the acquisition of Mannesmann Stainless Tubes after receiving the necessary authorizations. The company presents the deal as part of its industrial and strategic expansion in stainless steel and nickel alloys.

item.title: Steel Prices Fall Nearly 20% From Their Recent Peak
item.description: This video report says steel prices have fallen nearly 20% from a recent peak. It appears to focus on short-term price movement and broader business-market commentary.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Trading Economics reports nickel around $17,700 per tonne after a sharp decline from above $19,220. The move is framed as profit-taking by investors, which suggests recent volatility and weakening near-term momentum.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali has signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, a stainless steel scrap business in Italy. The company said the deal is intended to improve its environmental footprint and optimize operations.

item.title: PRESS RELEASE - Cogne Acciai Speciali
item.description: The company announced that Walsin Lihwa Corporation became the majority shareholder of Cogne Acciai Speciali after receiving all required clearances. The Marzorati family remains a long-term shareholder with a 30% stake.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali launches green hydrogen pilot plant
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali began operations at a green hydrogen pilot plant in Aosta, northern Italy. The project is part of the company’s broader sustainability and decarbonization efforts.

item.title: U.S. industry exceeds steel capacity utilization level outlined in Section 232 report
item.description: This article says U.S. steel capacity utilization has risen from 76% last April to above 80% last week, according to American Iron and Steel Institute data. It frames the increase as a threshold relevant to the Section 232 report’s capacity-utilization level.

item.title: Overcapacity will continue to weigh on steel industry
item.description: The piece reports that global steel overcapacity is expected to exceed 560 million tonnes this year, underscoring persistent pressure on the industry. It also notes MEPS’s forecast that global crude steel production will decline year on year in 2024.

item.title: Copper prices are surging amid geopolitical tensions, supply shortages and AI demand
item.description: The article says copper prices are rising sharply, driven by a combination of geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and strong demand tied to AI infrastructure. It also shows live commodity data for copper in India, indicating an elevated spot price and open interest levels.

item.title: Nickel rose to 17,964.50 USD/T on June 15, 2026, up 0.98% from the previous day
item.description: Nickel prices increased by 0.98% on June 15, 2026, reaching $17,964.50 per ton. Despite this daily gain, the metal's price has fallen 3.18% over the past month, though it remains 19.25% higher than its low point.

item.title: Nickel rose to 17,964.50 USD/T on June 15, 2026, up 0.98% from the previous day
item.description: Market data confirms Nickel's price rose to $17,964.50 USD/T on June 15, 2026. The report highlights a 0.98% daily increase against a 3.18% monthly decline, indicating short-term volatility within a longer-term recovery trend.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
item.description: Current trading data shows Nickel futures at $17,957.50, reflecting a daily increase. The article reiterates the bearish market sentiment due to a projected surplus through 2026, despite recent supply reductions.

item.title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
item.description: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a sustainable battery industry globally by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote energy efficiency. It emphasizes that ESG integration, stakeholder engagement, and robust reporting are essential for addressing environmental challenges in the nickel sector.

item.title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
item.description: This market update says U.S. steelmakers have excess mill capacity and may ramp up production, with output potentially surpassing 2021 levels. It also notes that recent production and capacity utilization data showed short-term stabilization, including a utilization rate of 77.5% in mid-May 2025.

item.title: Copper Rate in India Today Live
item.description: The Economic Times says copper prices are surging amid geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and AI-driven demand. It frames copper as a strategic commodity with markets anticipating a prolonged supply crunch because of underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects.

item.title: @HG.1: Copper (Jul'26) - Stock Price, Quote and News
item.description: CNBC’s copper quote page shows July 2026 copper futures trading with a high near 6.43 and an open around 6.388. The page is useful for tracking near-term market movement, though it does not provide a standalone article headline in the search result.

item.title: Copper price: Strains deepen as global smelting activity hits decade low
item.description: This article reports that global copper smelting activity has fallen to a decade low, with Earth-i’s SAVANT Global Copper Smelting Index showing 14.3% of global smelting capacity inactive in January. It also notes that copper prices have weakened, reflecting deeper strains in the market tied to reduced smelting activity and capacity utilization.

item.title: Copper Futures News - Investing.com
item.description: This news feed includes a Reuters item reporting that the United States imposed sanctions on an armed group aligned with illicit minerals activity in Congo, which is relevant to the copper supply chain. The page is a live copper news hub, so it also aggregates the latest market and mining headlines related to copper production and capacity.

item.title: Copper | Financial Times
item.description: The FT copper page highlights a $9 billion copper and gold project that has been pushed back because of worsening local security conditions tied to the Middle East war. That delay is relevant to copper production capacity because it affects project timelines and potential future supply.

item.title: Copper near record highs | MarketPulse by OANDA Group
item.description: This article says copper futures rose above USD 6.29 per pound, near record highs, on expectations of long-term demand and fears of supply constraints. While it focuses more on price than output, the supply-constraint framing is directly relevant to production and capacity conditions.

item.title: How Recycled Copper Plays a Pivotal Role in the Energy Transition
item.description: This article explains how recycled end-of-life copper can help meet rising demand for clean energy technologies. It highlights MIT modeling that suggests improved recycling technologies and rates could recover a much larger share of available scrap by 2040.

item.title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
item.description: EY discusses how copper companies are responding to supply pressure by increasing production, sustainability efforts, and digital innovation. The piece frames copper as a strategic material for the energy transition and broader industrial growth.

item.title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
item.description: S&P Global analyzes how copper demand is being driven by energy transition technologies, AI data centers, and electrification. It also discusses the supply gap and the role of recycling in increasing future copper availability.

item.title: The role of copper in the energy transition
item.description: DNV argues that copper scarcity could slow renewable energy adoption and sustainable development. The report recommends recycling, resource diversification, and technology innovation to address the supply-demand imbalance.

item.title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
item.description: The global nickel market is reaching a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge, potentially ending the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025. Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development, with Western Mines Group discovering a new large ultramafic deposit in Western Australia.

item.title: Nickel prices edged lower, then rose to around $17,941 per ton as market sentiment shifted
item.description: The article reports that global nickel prices rose 1.01% to $17,941.61 per ton on June 16, 2026, after edging slightly lower the previous day. It links the move to broader market conditions, including developments in global supply and geopolitical news affecting commodities.

item.title: Copper price today
item.description: Business Insider’s copper price page shows recent historical pricing, including June 15 and June 16 levels around 13,682. The page is focused more on price tracking than breaking news, but it helps contextualize recent market movement.

item.title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
item.description: Aluminum prices have gained 13% in 2025, with LME futures trading at three-year highs due to China's prevention of overcapacity and supply concerns from boxside grades. The video highlights a projected market deficit of 1.8 million tons in Europe and strong demand driving higher prices forward.

item.title: Aluminium Price Today
item.description: Aluminum futures are trading at $3,410.15, up from a previous close of $3,393.85, with the spot price at $3,408.50. The market shows continued upward pressure as China's Aluminum Corp fell 8.8% to HK$9.42 on Monday.

item.title: Steel production up 44.4% year over year after pandemic plunge
item.description: The video description says Great Lakes steel production rose by 5,000 tons last week and that U.S. steel mills remained close to 80% of capacity. It presents the gains as part of a broader rebound from the pandemic-era decline.

item.title: Aluminum Spot Price Chart
item.description: This page shows a recent aluminium spot price update at 3,419.16 USD per ton on June 17, 2026. It gives a near-real-time market reading rather than a narrative news story.

item.title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
item.description: The article says global nickel prices advanced 1.37% to $18,042.64 per ton, while Chinese spot markets also climbed. It frames the move as part of broader commodity and policy developments affecting supply and demand.

item.title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell by up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump plans to roll back some levies on aluminum and steel products. The Financial Times reported that the move is driven by concerns that the duties are hurting consumers, with aluminum trading at $3,030.00 per ton.

item.title: Breakthrough technologies
item.description: World Steel outlines the major technology categories for sustainable steelmaking, including carbon capture, hydrogen-based processes, biomass, and direct electrification. It notes that no single solution will fully decarbonize the sector and that a portfolio of technologies is required.

item.title: Italy’s Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff scheme
item.description: Prysmian said its business could benefit from recent U.S. copper tariff adjustments, which are expected to raise costs for cable importers. The company’s CEO said the new tariff scheme may support stronger pricing and margins for the Italian cable maker.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Trading Economics reports that steel prices fell to 3,084 CNY/T on June 18, 2026, down 0.06% from the previous day. It also notes that prices are down 3.08% over the past month but remain 4.37% higher over the past year, indicating mixed short-term and longer-term momentum.

item.title: Prysmian receives notice to proceed for Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector
item.description: Prysmian has officially received the notice to proceed for the construction of the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector, a key project for cross-border energy solutions. This press release, dated June 18, 2026, highlights the company's continued expansion in major European infrastructure projects[9].

item.title: Aluminum Prices Are Giving Up Some Gains Despite Gulf Disruptions - WSJ
item.description: This article says aluminum prices pulled back about 6% to $3,214.50 in early U.S. trading on Thursday after earlier gains. It links the move to softer demand, rising inventories in China, and pressure on Chinese buyers from higher costs, even as Middle East disruptions continue to affect supply chains.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Hit 6-Month High as Supply Concerns Grow
item.description: The article reports aluminum reaching a 6-month high as supply concerns intensified, especially around Chinese output nearing its production cap. It also notes the Midwest Premium hit an all-time high, reinforcing the view that tight supply is supporting prices.

item.title: Copper prices continue to surge amid geopolitical tensions, supply shortages, and AI-driven demand
item.description: Copper prices are rising on a combination of geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and strong demand tied to the AI boom. The article says traders are increasingly treating copper as a strategic commodity with a prolonged supply crunch expected from underinvestment and long project lead times.

item.title: Copper hit a fresh record high, but metals trade may be stretched
item.description: This segment says copper reached a fresh record high and crossed $13,000 a ton for the first time. It also raises the question of whether the metals rally has become overstretched after the rapid move higher.

item.title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
item.description: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, has been told to sharply reduce output. The report says the company was notified of a 12 million ton ore production quota for 2026, down from 42 million tons in 2025, as Indonesia seeks to support nickel prices.

item.title: NEW: Trump's tariffs credited for this MAJOR boost to US aluminum production
item.description: Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan details how President Trump's tariffs are credited with driving Century Aluminum's decision to significantly boost production. The report highlights a major shift in US aluminum output capacity linked directly to recent trade policies.

item.title: Boom-Bust-Bounce: Understanding Nickel's Latest Price Cycle
item.description: Global nickel prices retreated 2.45% on June 19, 2026, settling at $17,564.51 per ton, driven by Indonesia's decision to lower its benchmark nickel reference price signaling robust supply availability. Despite the drop, a realistic stable price range is likely to remain between $20,000 and $22,000 per tonne.

item.title: Aluminum Futures,Sep-2026 (ALI=F)
item.description: Yahoo Finance displays the latest delayed quote for September 2026 aluminum futures. The page reflects a recent rise in futures pricing and can help gauge current market sentiment, though it is not itself a news story.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Trading Economics reports that aluminum prices rose modestly on June 19, while monthly performance remained negative. The page combines price data with market news and trend tracking, making it relevant for recent aluminum market movement.

item.title: Current Challenges and Opportunities for the Aluminum Transformation Industry in the United States
item.description: The report identifies the two main challenges for the aluminum industry: reducing greenhouse gas emissions and increasing recycling rates. It explores strategic opportunities to address these issues through technological innovation and policy support.

item.title: New & Innovative Markets
item.description: A snapshot report shows that the U.S. aluminum industry has achieved positive environmental impact trends across nearly all measures over the past 25 years. The industry continues to innovate in new markets while improving sustainability.

item.title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
item.description: Recycling aluminum uses approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions. This efficiency makes aluminum a critical material for sustainable vehicle manufacturing across all segments.

item.title: Copper futures drop below $6.3/lb as Rio Tinto resumes Oyu Tolgoi exports
item.description: Copper futures fell below $6.3 per pound on Friday after giving back recent gains. The move followed news that Rio Tinto resumed exports of copper concentrate from its giant Oyu Tolgoi mine, which eased some supply concerns.

item.title: Global nickel prices retreat again as macro pressure weighs on base metals
item.description: This article says global nickel prices fell 1.42% on Jun 20, 2026 to $17,520.97 per ton. It links the decline to a stronger US dollar, the Federal Reserve holding interest rates steady, and easing trade bottlenecks tied to a pending US-Iran agreement.

item.title: In the week ending on June 20, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,851,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.2 percent
item.description: The American Iron and Steel Institute reported that U.S. raw steel production reached 1,851,000 net tons in the week ending June 20, 2026, with capability utilization at 80.2%, surpassing the administration's 80% target. This indicates a rebound in production efficiency and capacity use following earlier slowdowns.

item.title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
item.description: A new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year is nearly doubling current US domestic production, yet only six smelting operations remain with just four actively producing. Even at full capacity, US smelters could only satisfy about a third of domestic demand, requiring five years and $25 billion investment to meet current needs.

item.title: Copper futures price today
item.description: Investing.com shows copper futures trading at 6.3370, down from a previous close of 6.3855. The page provides live pricing and recent market information, making it useful for tracking near-term copper moves.

item.title: Copper Jul 26 (HG=F) stock price, news, quote & history
item.description: Yahoo Finance lists copper July 2026 futures at 6.385, with an intraday range of 6.270 to 6.385. The page also aggregates recent news and market data relevant to copper price trends.

item.title: Copper market news & forecasts
item.description: Fastmarkets says copper’s long-term outlook remains constrained by the industry’s limited ability to bring new supply online quickly enough to meet rising demand. The publication highlights permitting delays and supply constraints as key themes shaping the market.

item.title: Aosta, Cogne Acciai Speciali: bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
item.description: The article reports growing concern over Cogne Acciai Speciali’s future in Aosta after a sharp deterioration in its financial results. It says the company recorded a loss of 60.9 million euros in 2025, down from an 8 million euro profit the year before, amid U.S. tariffs, Asian competition, and higher energy costs.

item.title: Torna la cassa integrazione in Cogne Acciai Speciali
item.description: This piece reports that wage-support measures are returning at Cogne Acciai Speciali, affecting 750 employees for 13 weeks from June 9 to September 7. It frames the move as part of a difficult industrial situation for the company.

item.title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
item.description: Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful mining operations. The country is reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility as actual production continues to lag behind targets.

item.title: Nickel price spikes as risks to supply grow in top producer Indonesia
item.description: Nickel futures rallied sharply on the London Metal Exchange after reports that 10% to 15% of high-grade nickel pig iron capacity at Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park will undergo rotational maintenance. This supply risk in the world's top nickel producer has intensified market concerns about future availability.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,402.48 USD/T on June 22, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices were slightly higher in the latest market update, rising to 3,402.48 USD/T, a gain of 0.05% from the previous day. The data also shows the commodity has fallen 7.50% over the past month, despite remaining far above year-ago levels.

item.title: Aluminum Futures, Sep-2026 (ALI=F)
item.description: Yahoo Finance shows aluminum futures trading at 3,475.25, up 2.71% at the time of the quote. The market page suggests a stronger short-term move in futures compared with other commodity price trackers in the same period.

item.title: Copper futures hold near recent levels as market watches supply and demand signals
item.description: Copper futures were reported around $6.3 per pound on Monday, extending recent losses. The update attributed price pressure to resumed exports of copper concentrate from Rio Tinto's Oyu Tolgoi operations, alongside broader market attention on supply developments.

item.title: Copper prices surge on geopolitical tensions, supply shortages, and AI demand
item.description: The article says copper prices are up about 9% since the Iran war and are approaching a January peak. It highlights a mix of geopolitical risk, structural supply shortages, and AI-driven demand as key forces supporting the market.

item.title: Copper market outlook remains tight amid limited new supply
item.description: Fastmarkets says copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online quickly enough to meet demand. It points to permitting delays and broader supply-side bottlenecks as persistent market themes.

item.title: Nickel rose to 17,840.25 USD/T on June 22, 2026
item.description: Trading Economics reports that nickel gained 1.74% on June 22, 2026, indicating a firmer market in the latest session. It also notes that the metal is still down over the past month, showing recent volatility despite the day’s rebound.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Investing.com shows nickel futures trading around 17,550 to 17,701, with an intraday range between 17,489.50 and 17,765. The data suggests a relatively tight trading band in the latest session.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Home
item.description: Nucor announced on Monday, June 22, that its consumer spot price (CSP) for hot-rolled coil will be $1,130 per short ton for the week, an increase of $5/st from last week. This update reflects the latest spot pricing trends in the steel market.

item.title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
item.description: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum and Rio Tinto's use of hydrogen to replace natural gas in alumina refining.

item.title: Aluminium Spot Price Chart | Live Price of Aluminium per Ounce
item.description: Aluminum spot prices fell to $3,251.22 per ton on June 23, 2026, representing a 4.67% decline. The live price data highlights a significant drop in aluminum values, with the market showing continued weakness.

item.title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
item.description: Steel prices have continued to rise despite global conflicts, with hot-rolled coil reaching approximately $1,100 per ton. The typical steel pricing upcycle lasts 3–4 months, but the current nine-month upward trend suggests further increases, potentially reaching $1,500 per ton.

item.title: Steel Market Update: US sheet prices continue to move higher
item.description: US sheet steel prices are rising due to extremely limited spot availability, solid demand, and extended lead times. The market remains tight as suppliers struggle to meet growing customer orders.

item.title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
item.description: Steel manufacturers across the U.S. are planning over $6 billion in capital-spending projects set to begin in the second half of 2026, signaling near-term capacity growth. These investments aim to strengthen domestic production capabilities amid recovering demand and market stabilization.

item.title: Steel Production in the United States increased to 7500 Thousand Tonnes in May from 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April of 2026
item.description: U.S. steel production rose to 7.5 million tonnes in May 2026, up from 7.2 million tonnes in April, according to Trading Economics. The increase reflects a positive trend in domestic output, supported by recovering demand and improved mill utilization rates.

item.title: Total raw steel produced around the globe rebounded 3% in May to an estimated 157.9 million metric tons
item.description: Global raw steel production increased by 3% in May 2026, reaching an estimated 157.9 million metric tons, as reported by the World Steel Association. This rebound suggests a recovery in worldwide steel output amid stabilizing market conditions and rising demand.

item.title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
item.description: The 'Copper for Tomorrow' initiative aims to solve value chain challenges to increase sustainable copper production globally. This bid focuses on integrating circular economy principles and digital advancements to lead toward a greener future.

item.title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
item.description: Current hot-rolled coil prices stand at $1,109 per ton, with cold-rolled steel at $1,293 and coated steel at $1,380 per ton. Market analysts are evaluating whether these elevated prices will soon decline amid shifting supply dynamics.

item.title: HRC Steel fell to 1,193.04 USD/T on June 24, 2026
item.description: HRC Steel prices dropped slightly by 0.08% to $1,193.04 per ton on June 24, 2026, though they remain 1.88% higher over the past month and 34.20% higher than a year ago. The market shows resilience despite minor daily fluctuations.

item.title: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,070 per ton
item.description: Steel rebar futures declined below CNY 3,070 per ton, with prices falling 3.51% over the past month but still 4.41% higher than a year ago. This indicates a short-term dip within a longer-term upward trend.

item.title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
item.description: Nickel is crucial for the net-zero transition with demand expected to grow 65% this decade, making it imperative that production scales up with minimal environmental impact. A meta-analysis confirms that clean energy technologies are the fastest-growing sector for nickel demand, requiring the industry to adopt more sustainable practices.

item.title: Tenaris acquires control of Italy-based Fast
item.description: Luxembourg-based global steel pipe manufacturer Tenaris has announced the acquisition of control of Fast Srl, a Bergamo-based company that manufactures equipment and products for oil and gas extraction. This strategic move strengthens Tenaris's presence in the Italian industrial sector.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariffs
item.description: Milan-listed Prysmian, the world's largest cable maker, reported a 2.1% share increase following announcements of stronger U.S. pricing and margins due to new copper tariffs. Analysts at Citi suggest these tariffs will support pricing as the company benefits from domestic copper procurement amid U.S. tax adjustments on imported cables[1].
